heading: Reaffirmation of merit-based peer review
label: 1862s Reaffirmation of merit-based peer review
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the American Innovation and Competitiveness Act, and not as part of the National Science Foundation Act of 1950 which comprises this chapter.

Definitions, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–329, § 2130 Stat. 2970Jan. 6, 2017“In this Act [see Short Title of 2017 Amendment note set out under and Tables], unless expressly provided otherwise:section 1861 of this title“(1).—Appropriate committees of congressThe term ‘appropriate committees of Congress’ means the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives.“(2).—Federal science agencyThe term ‘Federal science agency’ has the meaning given the term in section 103 of the America COMPETES Reauthorization Act of 2010 ().42 U.S.C. 6623“(3).—FoundationThe term ‘Foundation’ means the National Science Foundation.“(4).—Institution of higher educationThe term ‘institution of higher education’ has the meaning given the term in section 101(a) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ().20 U.S.C. 1001(a)“(5).—NISTThe term ‘NIST’ means the National Institute of Standards and Technology.“(6).—STEMThe term ‘STEM’ has the meaning given the term in section 2 of the American [sic] COMPETES Reauthorization Act of 2010 [] ( note).Pub. L. 111–35842 U.S.C. 6621“(7).—STEM educationThe term ‘STEM education’ has the meaning given the term in section 2 of the STEM Education Act of 2015 [] ( note).”Pub. L. 114–5942 U.S.C. 6621

children.text: sustained, predictable Federal funding of basic research is essential to United States leadership in science and technology;
the Foundation’s intellectual merit and broader impacts criteria are appropriate for evaluating grant proposals, as concluded by the 2011 National Science Board Task Force on Merit Review;

children.label: A
children.text: the proposals funded by the Foundation are of high quality and advance scientific knowledge; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Foundation’s grants address societal needs through basic research findings or through related activities; and

as evidenced by the Foundation’s contributions to scientific advancement, economic growth, human health, and national security, its peer review and merit review processes have identified and funded scientifically and societally relevant basic research and should be preserved.

children.heading: Merit review criteria
children.label: b Merit review criteria
children.text: The Foundation shall maintain the intellectual merit and broader impacts criteria, among other specific criteria as appropriate, as the basis for evaluating grant proposals in the merit review process.

children.heading: Updates
children.label: c Updates
children.text: If after , a change is made to the merit-review process, the Director shall submit a report to the appropriate committees of Congress not later than 30 days after the date of the change.January 6, 2017
